---
title: "ScalePredSDM: Estimating scale of effect"
author: "Ole J. R. Sørensen"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{ScalePredSDM: Estimating scale of effect}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)
```


```{r}
library(ScalePredSDM)
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(ggplot2)
```


#Create virtual predictors - Depth and some rare habitat for this example
```{r}
# patchy continuous predictor (e.g. depth), no monotonic gradient
r1 <- rast(
  nrows = 50, ncols = 50,
  xmin = 0, xmax = 1000,
  ymin = 0, ymax = 1000,
  crs  = "EPSG:32636"
)

set.seed(10)

# base random field
values(r1) <- rnorm(ncell(r1))

# multi-scale smoothing to induce patchiness
r1_s1 <- terra::focal(r1, w = matrix(1, 5, 5),  fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)
r1_s2 <- terra::focal(r1, w = matrix(1, 11, 11), fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)

# combine scales
r1 <- 0.6 * r1_s1 + 0.4 * r1_s2

# rescale for readability
r1 <- terra::scale(r1)
names(r1) <- "depth"

# rare, patchy binary habitat
r2 <- rast(
  nrows = 50, ncols = 50,
  xmin = 0, xmax = 1000,
  ymin = 0, ymax = 1000,
  crs  = "EPSG:32636"
)

#set.seed(42)
values(r2) <- runif(ncell(r2))

# smooth to create spatial patches
w <- matrix(1, nrow = 9, ncol = 9)
r2_smooth <- terra::focal(r2, w = w, fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)

# threshold to make habitat rare (~15%)
thr <- quantile(values(r2_smooth), probs = 0.90, na.rm = TRUE)
r2 <- as.numeric(r2_smooth >= thr)

names(r2) <- "habitat_binary"
```
#Generate some random survey points

```{r}
#set.seed(24)

n_points <- 50
pts <- data.frame(
  x = runif(n_points, 0, 1000),
  y = runif(n_points, 0, 1000)
)

pts_sf <- st_as_sf(pts, coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 32636)

```

#Simmulate abundance that is somewhat correlated with the rare habitat
```{r}
#Simulate abundance that increases near rare habitat patches
# (i.e. a spatial neighbourhood effect, not point-only)

# distance to nearest habitat cell
dist_to_habitat <- terra::distance(r2 == 1)

# extract distance at survey points
d <- terra::extract(dist_to_habitat, terra::vect(pts_sf))[, 2]

#set.seed(5)
# Abundance was simulated as a distance-decay function from rare habitat patches, with effect size chosen to ensure a clear neighbourhood-scale signal.

mu <- 5 + 12 * exp(-d / 100)

pts_sf$abundance <- round(
  mu + rnorm(length(mu), mean = 0, sd = 0.5)
)

pts_sf$abundance[pts_sf$abundance < 0] <- 0
```


#Assemble the predictor stack
```{r}
predictors <- c(r1, r2)
names(predictors) <- c("depth", "habitat_binary")

```

#Estimate the scale of effect for each predictor
```{r}
res <- estimate_SoE_correlations(
  predictors   = predictors,
  points_sf    = pts_sf,
  response_col = "abundance",
  scales_m     = c(50, 100, 150, 200),
  fun_by_pred  = c(
    depth = "mean",
    habitat_binary = "sum"
  ),
  return_plot = TRUE
)

res$plot

```

#Assemble a Scale of Effect predictor stack
```{r}
SoE_table <- res$best_scales[, c("predictor", "scale")]
names(SoE_table)[2] <- "scale_m"

soe_stack <- build_SoE_stack_fast(
  predictors  = predictors,
  SoE_table   = SoE_table,
  fun_by_pred = c(
    depth = "mean",
    habitat_binary = "sum"
  ),
  quiet = TRUE
)


combined_stack <- c(predictors, soe_stack)

plot(combined_stack)
```
